workflows.draw_freehand: Select the Pencil tool from the Tools group (or press P). Click and drag on the canvas to draw lines.
workflows.draw_shape: Click on a shape in the Shapes group (rectangle, ellipse, line, triangle, etc.). Click and drag on the canvas to place it. Drag handles to resize.
workflows.add_text: Click the Text tool (A icon) in the Tools group. Click on the canvas where you want text. A text box appears — type your text. Use the formatting toolbar above to change font and size.
workflows.fill_color: Click the Fill tool (paint bucket icon). Select a color from the Colors palette. Click on an enclosed area to fill it.
workflows.select_color: Click a color in the Colors palette on the right side of the ribbon. Left-click sets foreground color, right-click sets background color.
workflows.erase: Click the Eraser tool. Click and drag on the canvas to erase.
workflows.save_as: Press Ctrl+Shift+S. Choose format (PNG, JPEG, BMP). Type filename. Click Save.

shortcuts.text: T (or click A icon in Tools)

layout.ribbon: Below title bar — contains tool groups: Selection, Image, Tools, Brushes, Shapes, Size, Colors
layout.tools_group: Contains: Select, Pencil, Fill, Text (A), Eraser, Color Picker, Magnifier
layout.shapes_group: Contains: Line, Rectangle, Ellipse, Triangle, Arrow, Star, etc.
layout.colors_palette: Right side of ribbon — clickable color swatches
layout.canvas: The large WHITE area below the ribbon — this is where you draw
layout.status_bar: Bottom — shows cursor position and canvas size

tips: The canvas is the large white rectangle below the toolbar. ALWAYS draw on the canvas, not the toolbar.
Before drawing, select the right tool FIRST (Pencil for freehand, a Shape for geometric drawing).
For drawing a house: use Rectangle shape for the body, Triangle for the roof, small Rectangle for door/windows.
For freehand drawing: press P for Pencil, then drag on the canvas.
The Text tool (A) requires two clicks: one on the tool, one on the canvas to place the text box.
Select a larger brush size from the Size dropdown for thicker lines.
